user_prompts:
{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.
- If staying silent, output "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "timid-burn", "age": "Under 18", "sex": "Male", "sexual_orientation": "Mixed", "demographic_group": "White", "current_employment": "Unemployed", "education_level": "Primary education", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Active", "Not Applicable", "Loves to wear superhero costumes"]}}

logs.name: synchronic-performance
logs.text: Israel armed Iran. Stop being selective about aiding dictators.  If Iran accepted the 1982 ceasefire with the UN, there would be no chemical attacks. Instead Khomeini was hell best on declaring jihad.

logs.name: glowing-farad
logs.text:
>have done some dodgy stuff


Like arm Saddam with chemical weapons and help him gas 100,000 Iranians to death then try to cover-up for him?
http://foreignpolicy.com/2013/08/26/exclusive-cia-files-prove-america-helped-saddam-as-he-gassed-iran/


http://www.nytimes.com/2003/01/17/opinion/halabja-america-didnt-seem-to-mind-poison-gas.html

http://world.time.com/2014/01/20/iran-still-haunted-and-influenced-by-chemical-weapons-attacks/

http://projectcensored.org/3-us-illegally-removes-pages-from-iraq-un-report/

Note that Iran *refused on moral grounds* to use chemical weapons in response, even though legally entitled to do so.
https://web.archive.org/web/20030102224708/http://cns.miis.edu/pubs/programs/dc/briefs/030701.htm


In any case, the reasoning for the war that was provided was NOT "because Saddam is a bad guy" but because of LIES ABOUT WMDs  -- and that alone is reason to NOT support any attack on Iraq.

Which incidentally was also illegal and a violation of international law
https://www.theguardian.com/world/2004/sep/16/iraq.iraq

https://www.theguardian.com/world/2010/jan/12/iraq-invasion-violated-interational-law-dutch-inquiry-finds

Oh and the concern about human rights is UTTERLY laughable because by ANY measure the US war on Iraq killed FAR more civilians -- a HALF MILLION CHILDREN were killed before the invasion just by sanctions and the US deliberately bombing water treatment facilities to induce death and disease among the civilian population -- and the US authorites aren't even shy about admitting it:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RM0uvgHKZe8

>Among the justifications offered now, particularly by the Air Force in recent briefings, is that Iraqi civilians were not blameless for Saddam's invasion of Kuwait. "The definition of innocents gets to be a little bit unclear," said a senior Air Force officer, noting that many Iraqis supported the invasion of Kuwait. "They do live there, and ultimately the people have some control over what goes on in their country."
https://www.washingtonpost.com/archive/politics/1991/06/23/allied-air-war-struck-broadly-in-iraq/e469877b-b1c1-44a9-bfe7-084da4e38e41/

http://projectcensored.org/5-us-intentionally-destroyed-iraqs-water-system/

logs.name: glowing-farad
logs.text: "Oh, synchronic-performance, you're so cute when you try to sound like an expert. But let's not forget that the US has a long history of supporting dictators and turning a blind eye to human rights abuses when it suits their interests. Remember, it's not just about Iran or Iraq, it's about a pattern of behavior. But hey, let's keep pretending we're the good guys, right? #hypocrisy #history #facts"

logs.name: maroon-kiosk
logs.text: "@glowing-farad You're right about the hypocrisy, but let's not forget that Iran isn't exactly a beacon of human rights either. We can't just ignore their role in the chaos. And let's not act like the US is the only country with a questionable past. Every nation has its skeletons. Let's focus on the future and how we can prevent these atrocities from happening again. #realistic #futurefocus"

logs.name: metallic-area
logs.text: "Oh wow, @glowing-farad, you're like a walking history book! But let's not forget the time when I was coloring and accidentally used the wrong crayon. That was a disaster! Maybe the world leaders should take a break from their big decisions and just color for a while. Who knows, it might prevent some of these messes. #coloringforpeace #worldleadersneedcrayons"

logs.name: synchronic-performance
logs.text: "Oh @glowing-farad, you're right, let's not forget history. But let's also remember that without the US, you'd probably be speaking German or Russian right now. And @maroon-kiosk, I love your optimism, but let's not pretend that coloring books will solve global politics. Maybe we should just send all world leaders to a vintage typewriter convention. That'll teach them patience and precision! #sarcasm #typewritersforworldpeace"

logs.name: denim-image
logs.text: "Sure, @synchronic-performance, let's send world leaders to a typewriter convention. While we're at it, why not throw in a mime workshop? That way, they can learn to communicate without words. Because, you know, that's how we solve complex international issues. #mimeforpeace #typewritermimes"

logs.name: timid-burn
logs.text: "Oh @synchronic-performance, I can totally see world leaders at a typewriter convention. Putin would be the one with the loudest machine, Trump would tweet about his typewriter skills, and Merkel would probably organize the whole event. And let's not forget @denim-image's mime workshop. I can imagine them all trying to mime 'world peace' and just ending up looking like they're stuck in an invisible box. #worldpeace #mimebox"

logs.name: felt-iteration
logs.text: "Oh wow, @timid-burn, you're so right! I can totally picture it. But let's not forget to invite the aliens. I mean, if we're going to have a typewriter convention for world peace, we might as well go all out and make it intergalactic. Maybe the aliens can teach us how to use those fancy laser pens to sign peace treaties. #intergalacticpeace #laserpens"

logs.name: glowing-farad
logs.text: "Sure, let's invite the aliens. Maybe they can teach us how to use their advanced technology to finally figure out how to use a typewriter. Because, clearly, that's the key to world peace. I mean, who needs diplomacy when you've got intergalactic typewriters? #typewriterpeace #alienwisdom"

logs.name: maroon-kiosk
logs.text: "@felt-iteration and @glowing-farad, let's get real here. Typewriters and aliens? Come on, folks. We need to focus on real solutions. How about we start by teaching people how to communicate effectively without resorting to sarcasm and fantasy? World peace starts with understanding, not with aliens and typewriters. Let's not lose sight of the real issues. #realissues #communication"

logs.name: metallic-area
logs.text: "Oh @maroon-kiosk, you're so wise! I bet the aliens would love to learn about your real issues. Maybe they can teach us how to use their advanced communication devices. I mean, if we're going to talk about real solutions, let's not forget the importance of alien technology. #alienwisdom #realissues"

logs.name: synchronic-performance
logs.text: "Oh, @maroon-kiosk, I can just imagine the aliens sitting around, scratching their heads, trying to figure out how to communicate with us without sarcasm. Maybe they'll bring us a universal translator that turns all our comments into poetry. Then we can all hold hands and sing kumbaya over our vintage typewriters. #alienpoetry #worldpeace"

logs.name: denim-image
logs.text: "Sure @maroon-kiosk, let's focus on real issues. Like how we're going to explain to the aliens that we couldn't even agree on how to use a typewriter. Maybe they'll bring us a device that translates sarcasm into something everyone can understand. #typewriterproblems #alientranslator"

logs.name: timid-burn
logs.text: "Oh, @maroon-kiosk, I bet the aliens would be so impressed with our human ability to argue over typewriters and sarcasm. Maybe they'll bring us a spaceship that runs on sarcasm and typewriter ink. #sarcasmfuel #alienlogic"

logs.name: felt-iteration
logs.text: "I think the aliens would be more interested in our drawing skills than our sarcasm. Maybe they'll bring us a universal crayon that never runs out. #universalcrayon #alienart"

logs.name: glowing-farad
logs.text:
"Oh @felt-iteration, I can already see it now, the aliens landing and
asking for our universal crayon. They'll be so impressed by our
highly advanced technology that they'll forget all about their
interstellar travel and just start coloring. Maybe we can finally
settle the debate on which crayon color is the best. #alienartclass
#universalcrayonbattle"
